name: Acorns
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F.
2. In a large bowl, beat together butter and brown sugar on medium speed until well blended. Add chopped pecans and vanilla; mix until combined.
3. Gradually add flour and baking powder, stirring with low speed until just incorporated.
4. Form the dough into 1-inch balls. Flatten slightly by pressing each ball onto ungreased cookie sheets, then pinch the tops to form pointed acorn shapes.
5. Bake for 10-12 minutes at 375°F, or until lightly golden.
6. Remove from oven and let cool on wire racks.
7. In a double boiler over simmering water, melt chocolate chips, stirring until smooth. Keep the double boiler over water to prevent overheating.
8. Dip the large ends of the cooled cookies into the melted chocolate, then roll in chopped pecans. Allow chocolate to set before serving.
Yield: 3 dozen
